company.name: Pixellot
company.tagline: Why buy a camera when you can own the future?
company.mission_statement: Revolutionizing the world of sports with pioneering AI-automated solutions for capturing, producing, streaming, analyzing and monetizing games.
company.value_proposition: Pixellot enables sports organizations, media companies, leagues, schools, and clubs to automate the entire video production workflow — filming, streaming, analysis, and monetization — across 19 sport types, with no production staff required. It eliminates the cost and complexity of traditional camera crews by replacing them with AI-automated, fixed or portable camera systems.
company.headquarters: Israel (founded by Gal Oz and Dr. Miky Tamir; global offices not individually listed on site)

scale_stats.games_broadcasted: 5,152,646 total games produced (homepage live counter; ~163,185 games per month)
scale_stats.hours_of_video: Over 90,000 hours of live content produced monthly (per FAQ reliability claim)
scale_stats.leagues_partners: Not specified as a single number; partners span federations, leagues, broadcasters, and clubs across 80+ countries
scale_stats.users_or_athletes: 10,000+ clubs and teams using Pixellot Air alone

awards_recognition: 2025 — Partnership of the Year, STA Group
2024 — Social Impact Award (ISE Awards); STA Power List; Best Tech Company (SportsPro OTT); BaM Award (NAB Show)
2023 — VideoTech Innovation Award; Top 100 Next Scale-Ups (VivaTech); 10 Most Innovative Sports Tech Companies (Sports Business Journal)
2022 — NAB Next TV Best in Market
2019 — NAB Product of the Year; 15 Most Promising Startups

2018 — Entrepreneurial Company of the Year (Frost & Sullivan); Gartner Cool Vendor in AI for Media & Entertainment; Top 20 Hottest Israeli Startups; Best Israeli Startup in Social & Media (Atlas Award)

fixed_camera_systems.target_audience: Clubs, schools, federations, leagues, media platforms, broadcasters, venues, and coaching organizations at all competitive levels
fixed_camera_systems.description: An end-to-end automated sports production and streaming system designed for organizations that want minimal operational overhead. Sport-specific AI algorithms automatically track the action, produce a broadcast feed, generate highlights, and deliver live and on-demand content — all without a human operator.
key_features: Unmanned, fully-automated sports tracking camera
Indoor and outdoor capable
Low minimum bandwidth required — 3G/4G supported
Audio inputs for on-premise or remote commentary
Auto-adjusts to poor lighting conditions
Embedded graphics and ad format overlays
Automatic highlights and condensed game summaries
Live and on-demand playback with rewind capability
Free companion app for fans — live viewing, clip creation, sharing

camera_specs.lens_type: Multi-camera wide-angle array stitched into panoramic view
camera_specs.coverage_area: Full field/court coverage for all four corners from midfield/midcourt elevation
camera_specs.installation_type: Permanent ceiling/rail mount at midfield; computer tower can be up to 100m+ away from camera head

ai_features: Sport-specific AI algorithms calibrated per discipline
Automated pan-tilt-zoom replicating a professional camera operator
Automatic highlight generation and condensed match summaries
AI-driven light recognition and exposure adjustment
fixed_camera_systems.connectivity: 3G/4G cellular or fixed-line internet; minimum 2 MB/s upload for live streaming, preferred 5–10 MB/s; RTMP, RTSP, SDI, NDI, API, and SDK output supported; up to 7 simultaneous stream variants
unique_selling_points: Zero human operators required after installation
Over 30,000 systems deployed globally
One installation covers multiple sports and teams across an entire season
Reduces production costs to a minimum vs. traditional crews
Embedded monetization and sponsorship formats built in

fixed_camera_systems.tagline: Broadcast-quality automated production for professional and semi-professional sports
fixed_camera_systems.target_audience: Professional leagues, broadcasters, media companies, and semi-professional organizations requiring broadcast-grade output
fixed_camera_systems.description: Pixellot's top-tier fixed camera system, delivering broadcast-quality full HD production at 50/60 FPS. Supports both manual and automatic production modes, enabling professional broadcasters to add multi-angle coverage with a fraction of the traditional crew. Designed for environments where broadcast standards are non-negotiable.

Manual and automatic production modes
Multi-angle support requiring only one operator instead of a full crew
Remote commentary integration
Embedded live graphics with automated or manual scoreboard updates
Multiple stream variant outputs

fixed_camera_systems.connectivity: Fixed-line preferred; RTMP, RTSP, SDI, NDI, API, SDK output; 1080i interlaced conversion supported for legacy broadcast
unique_selling_points: Broadcast-grade 50/60 FPS output — same quality as traditional outside broadcast trucks

fixed_camera_systems.tagline: The world's first automated multi-camera production solution for baseball
fixed_camera_systems.target_audience: High school baseball programs, collegiate leagues, amateur baseball associations, and venues lacking infrastructure
fixed_camera_systems.description: An affordable, end-to-end automated baseball solution delivering live AI-driven coverage from both the batter and pitcher's sides of the field simultaneously. Weatherproof and battery-operated, it works in challenging light and power-limited venues.

Covers both batter and pitcher perspectives
Follows the action automatically — no video operator needed
Weatherproof and works in different lighting conditions
Easy-to-use mobile app
Power over Ethernet (PoE)
Dynamic video production — captures fence and point-of-view angles

camera_specs.installation_type: Portable, battery-operated; deployable at venues without stable power or internet

mobile_camera_systems.tagline: Portable AI-powered sports camera for clubs, academies, and coaches
mobile_camera_systems.target_audience: Youth clubs, academies, colleges, high schools, referees, semi-professional organizations, and coaches requiring a portable solution
mobile_camera_systems.description: A lightweight, portable AI-powered sports camera that automates capture, production, streaming, and analysis from any venue. Placed at the halfway line, it provides a full tactical panoramic view without requiring a camera operator. Includes in-app coding, tagging, player analytics, and instant post-game cloud availability.

Auto-production and automatic highlights
Automatic streaming during matches or training sessions
Post-game footage instantly available via cloud for coding and tagging
In-app coding, tagging, annotation, and clip sharing
Full pitch tactical panoramic view covering all field players
Team and player analytics — data visualization, shot charts, heatmaps
Compatible with third-party analytics platforms

hardware_specs.portability: Fully portable; mountable on tripod or temporary fixture for any indoor or outdoor venue

ai_features: AI Director mode — auto-production with no human intervention

mobile_camera_systems.tagline: Software-based automated production using your own action camera
mobile_camera_systems.target_audience: Amateur and semi-professional organizations, individual coaches, referees, and teams wanting AI automation without proprietary hardware investment
mobile_camera_systems.description: A software solution that brings Pixellot's AI automation to any wide-angle action camera. Users mount their own off-the-shelf action camera on a tripod, and Pixellot You handles tracking, production, and streaming automatically — eliminating the need for Pixellot-branded hardware.
key_features: Works with user's own wide-angle action camera
AI-automated tracking and production
Fully mobile — tripod-mounted anywhere
No proprietary hardware required

hardware_specs.portability: Completely portable — camera on tripod, no fixed installation

ai_features: AI automated tracking via software overlay on existing camera hardware
sports_supported: and other sports supported by Pixellot's software algorithms
mobile_camera_systems.connectivity: Wi-Fi or cellular; cloud upload after session
unique_selling_points: Lowest cost entry point into the Pixellot ecosystem
No proprietary camera purchase required
Ideal for budget-constrained amateur programs

software_and_platforms.target_audience: Federations, leagues, broadcasters, media companies, clubs, and sports venues seeking to own their branded streaming channel

Supports live streaming, on-demand content, advanced stats, and eight distinct monetization models. Can be deployed and streaming within 48 hours of setup.

Platform-native formats (9:16, 16:9) generated automatically

technology_and_ai.overview: Pixellot's patented AI-automation technology combines fixed multi-camera arrays with sport-specific computer vision algorithms to produce a broadcast-quality video feed without any human operator. The system continuously captures a panoramic full-field view, runs real-time AI analysis to detect the ball and players, then automatically produces dynamic zooms and pans that replicate the output of a professional broadcast crew. Over 90,000 hours of live content produced monthly continuously feeds the self-learning AI.

computer_vision.description: Multiple cameras stitch a panoramic view of the entire playing surface. Sport-specific CV algorithms analyze the scene in real time to detect ball position, player movements, and game events. The AI then selects the optimal virtual crop window and generates dynamic zooms and pans to produce a broadcast-grade output stream.
tracking_capabilities: Ball detection and tracking across 19 sport types
Player position and movement tracking across the full field
Automatic zoom and pan generation based on action center
Event detection (goals, baskets, key plays) for highlight triggers
Lighting variation detection with automatic exposure adjustment
Play-type tagging and searchable clip library (via VidSwap)
Shot charts and heatmaps for player and team analysis
Offside-line inference via full-pitch panoramic view (soccer)
computer_vision.model_training: Sport-specific AI models trained on 90,000+ hours of live content produced monthly. Pixellot describes its AI as self-learning, continuously improved by the volume of games processed. Models are separately tuned per sport type across all 19 disciplines. FC Barcelona's coaching staff provided direct input on the coaching analytics model.

ai_director.description: The AI Director is 'the patented technological heart of Pixellot solutions' — software running on a local computer tower connected to the fixed camera array that automatically decides camera angle, zoom level, and panning behavior throughout a game without human input.
ai_director.how_it_works: 1. The full-field panoramic view is continuously captured by the multi-camera array. 2. Sport-specific CV algorithms analyze the panoramic frame in real time to locate the ball and players. 3. The AI Director selects the optimal virtual crop window — zoom level, angle, and pan speed — based on action location. 4. The resulting stream is encoded and distributed via RTMP, RTSP, SDI, NDI, API, or SDK. 5. Up to 7 simultaneous stream variants (different resolutions, bitrates, with or without graphics) are produced from a single capture.
ai_director.output_quality: Broadcast-grade production without operators. Pixellot Prime delivers full HD at 50/60 FPS. All systems support 1080p; 1080i interlaced conversion available for legacy broadcast infrastructure. Audio is captured via built-in microphones with support for wired/wireless auxiliary mics and remote commentary feeds.

auto_clipping_and_highlights.description: Pixellot Automatic Highlights uses AI to identify the most impactful game moments in real time and post-match, compiling them into multiple formats without any manual editing. The system simultaneously produces full recaps, social-ready short clips, and vertical formats for Stories and Reels.
trigger_events: Key plays broadly across all supported sports
Game's most thrilling moments as identified by sport-specific AI event detection
auto_clipping_and_highlights.delivery_format: Real-time short clips published to social media accounts (X, Instagram, TikTok) during the game; 2–10 minute mid-length recap available shortly after the game; full highlight compilation within 4 hours post-match; vertical/portrait 9:16 format auto-generated for Reels, Shorts, and TikTok; syndication-ready files for media publisher distribution.

cloud_architecture.description: Pixellot uses a cloud-based workflow where the local camera-and-server unit handles real-time AI processing and encoding, then uploads footage and streams to Pixellot's cloud infrastructure for storage, further processing, and delivery to end users.
cloud_architecture.upload_pipeline: Camera array → local computer tower (AI processing, encoding) → cloud upload via internet connection → Pixellot cloud servers → end-user delivery via OTT app, web platform, or third-party broadcast distribution (RTMP/RTSP/SDI/NDI/API/SDK). Footage is available on-demand immediately after upload. In low-connectivity situations, local recording is preserved and uploaded when connectivity is restored.
cloud_architecture.latency: Live streaming requires minimum 2 MB/s upload (preferred 5–10 MB/s); 3G/4G cellular streaming supported. Specific end-to-end latency figures are not publicly disclosed.
cloud_architecture.scalability: 30,000+ active systems simultaneously operating across 80+ countries. The cloud architecture supports simultaneous live production from all deployed venues. Pixellot OTT platform is ready for streaming within 48 hours of deployment.
cloud_providers: AWS (listed as an official partner on the Pixellot homepage partner strip)

data_and_analytics.description: Beyond video, Pixellot generates rich performance data through its VidSwap/Advantage analytics layer. All games are automatically pre-tagged and broken down within 4–8 hours, providing coaches and organizations with searchable clip libraries, statistical dashboards, and visual analytics.
data_outputs: Shot charts per player and team
Heatmaps of player positioning and movement
Tagged video clips by play type, situation, and player
Team and individual athlete performance statistics
Live scoreboard and in-game graphics data
Business intelligence: user, video, client, and operational insights (OTT)
data_and_analytics.api_availability: Yes — Pixellot supports RTMP, RTSP, SDI, NDI, API, and SDK output for third-party integration. Live data can be ingested by partners for betting, scoring, and analytics. Sportradar and Genius Sports are listed as official partners, indicating data feed partnerships.
data_and_analytics.data_format: Internal proprietary format; compatible with external partners including Sportradar, Stats Perform, and Genius Sports. VidSwap accepts uploads from any video source including exports from Hudl.

pricing_and_business_model.model_type: Hardware + subscription (Camera-as-a-Service). Customers acquire the Pixellot camera system (purchase or financing) and pay an ongoing subscription for the software, cloud, streaming, and analytics components. The OTT platform is a separate SaaS layer. Pixellot describes its overall offering as 'Camera-as-a-Service'.
pricing_and_business_model.hardware_cost_indication: No public pricing listed on the website. All hardware and subscription inquiries are directed to the sales team via the contact form. U.S. high schools can access Pixellot hardware at no cost through the NFHS Network partnership.

subscription_tiers.price: Contact sales — hardware may be subsidized via NFHS Network for US schools
included_features: Pixellot Show or Air NXT automated camera
subscription_tiers.target_segment: High schools, youth teams, amateur clubs

included_features: Advanced VidSwap analytics (FC Barcelona-grade coaching tools)
subscription_tiers.target_segment: Professional clubs, leagues, federations, broadcasters, media companies

pricing_and_business_model.contract_length: Not publicly disclosed. CCAA signed a five-year deal; NFHS Network is a long-term partnership. Contracts likely annual or multi-year based on published case studies.
pricing_and_business_model.free_trial_or_demo: Demo available on request through the website contact form ('Let's Talk' / 'Speak with a Sales Expert'). No self-serve free trial is mentioned.

vs_traditional_camera_crew.objection: Why use an AI camera instead of hiring a professional camera operator?
response_points: Automated systems are typically far more affordable — one setup covers an entire season across multiple teams and sports vs. hiring a crew per game
Zero human operators required after a one-time installation taking roughly one day
Eliminates scheduling complexity — no need to find and book a crew for every event
Scales effortlessly — 30,000+ systems operate simultaneously globally, which no crew model can match
Output is broadcast-grade (Pixellot Prime at 50/60 FPS) rivaling professional outside broadcast trucks
Once installed it 'just works' — no per-game logistics or operator fatigue

vs_manual_recording.objection: Can't a coach or parent just record on a phone or tripod camera?
response_points: Pixellot provides a full broadcast-quality output with automated zooms and pans, not a static wide shot
Footage is automatically uploaded to the cloud, tagged, broken down, and analytics-ready within 4–8 hours
Automatic highlight generation, social media publishing, and player showreels are produced with zero effort
Live streaming to any device for remote family members is built in — phone recordings cannot be streamed live at scale
VidSwap pre-tagging and coaching analytics cannot be replicated from a manual phone recording
Player recruitment showreels and statistical analysis are generated automatically

vs_competitors.objection: How is Pixellot different from Hudl Focus, Veo, or Trace?
differentiation_points: Pixellot is fully automated end-to-end; Veo requires manual clipping post-game
Hudl often requires separate filming hardware; Pixellot is a complete integrated system
Pixellot is the only solution offering a full white-label OTT platform for monetization
Pixellot scales from high school to professional broadcast (Pixellot Prime at 50/60 FPS) — competitors do not
Pixellot supports 19 sport types with sport-specific AI models; most competitors focus on soccer and basketball
Film sharing with Hudl users is supported bidirectionally — organizations can migrate without losing existing archives
FIBA official product approval — the only AI sports camera with federation-level endorsement for basketball
Pixellot's AI is trained on 90,000+ hours of live content per month, providing the largest sports-specific training dataset
Full analytics stack (VidSwap/Advantage) designed with FC Barcelona — not available from commodity camera competitors

setup_complexity.objection: Is the system hard to install and maintain?
response_points: Full permanent installation completes in one day; calibration takes 15–20 minutes and only needs to be done once
Minimal installation requirements: an internet connection (min 5 Mb/s upload) and electricity
Computer tower can be located up to 100m+ away from the camera head — no complex cabling at the camera mount point
Pixellot You and Air NXT are portable solutions set up in minutes on a standard tripod
Maintenance is 'set and forget' — occasional lens cleaning and rigging integrity checks only
No dedicated technical staff required to operate the system day-to-day
'Once the unit is installed and games are scheduled – it just works!'

budget_concerns.objection: This seems expensive for a small school or club.
response_points: U.S. high schools can access Pixellot hardware through the NFHS Network at no upfront cost
One system covers an entire school's athletics program across multiple sports and teams — far cheaper than hiring crews per game
The system generates revenue through built-in sponsorship, advertising, and OTT subscription tools, often offsetting the investment
Pixellot You uses the organization's own existing action camera — no hardware purchase required
Amateur and youth pricing tiers exist; contact sales for a customized budget-appropriate package
ROI is measurable: 8,000 US high schools now broadcast games that previously had zero coverage or revenue

ai_accuracy.objection: How accurate is the AI tracking? Will it lose the ball?
response_points: Over 90,000 hours of live content produced monthly continuously improves the self-learning AI
Sport-specific models are separately calibrated for each of the 19 supported sports — not a generic one-size-fits-all algorithm
More than 5 million games produced on the platform provide an unrivaled training dataset
If internet or power are lost, local recording is preserved — no game footage is permanently lost
Built-in light recognition adjusts the picture automatically when lighting conditions change
FIBA has officially approved Pixellot's basketball product, providing third-party validation of tracking quality
Systems have been used at elite professional level including FC Barcelona, La Liga, and all Mexican professional league clubs

testimonials.quote: The best thing about the technology is that once the unit is installed and games are scheduled – it just works!
testimonials.author_title: Representative, US high school sports program (8,000 schools via NFHS Network)

testimonials.quote: Pixellot was a game-changer for us.
testimonials.related_product: Pixellot Show / Air NXT for youth handball streaming

testimonials.quote: An all-in-one system that could handle production and broadcasting, was reliable and field-proven.
testimonials.related_product: Pixellot Show for collegiate sports production

case_studies.challenge: Over 8,000 high schools lacked the budget or staff to broadcast their sporting events, leaving fans unable to watch and athletic departments generating no streaming revenue.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot Show installed across ~8,000 schools via NFHS Network partnership, with hardware provided at no cost to schools.
results: 8,000+ US high schools now broadcasting games that previously had zero coverage
Schools generating sponsorship and subscription revenue for the first time
Revenue sharing for schools increased effective August 1, 2020

case_studies.challenge: needed scalable, affordable streaming solution to reach a broad school sports audience in Africa
case_studies.solution: Pixellot OTT platform with automated production

case_studies.challenge: Needed an affordable, scalable production solution to cover all sports across a multi-school conference.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot Show with a multi-year conference-wide agreement.
results: Five-year streaming partnership covering all CCAA collegiate sports

case_studies.challenge: Needed to improve team and player performance analysis across all league levels including youth, while making coverage affordable at scale.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot AI production and VidSwap analytics deployed across Israeli football structure.
results: Automated performance improvement tools before and during matches
Landmark partnership announced for grassroots development

case_studies.customer_name: MLB Draft League / San Diego League
case_studies.challenge: Needed affordable automated multi-camera baseball coverage across multiple venues, some lacking stable power and internet.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot DoublePlay installed at 17 MLB Draft League venues, Appalachian League, and San Diego League sites.
results: Coverage achieved at fields lacking stable power and internet infrastructure

case_studies.challenge: Needed automated coverage of all academy and youth games including away matches, which was cost-prohibitive with traditional crews.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot Air NXT deployed across all age groups.
results: All games at all levels and age groups captured, including away games

case_studies.challenge: Needed to cover a large multi-sport international event with many simultaneous venues.
COVID-19 prevented in-person attendance; parents and fans could not watch youth handball games.
case_studies.solution: Pixellot automated streaming deployed for live broadcast of youth games.

sales_and_contact.primary_cta: Let's Talk / Speak with a Sales Expert (contact form)

regional_offices.contact_detail: Global team spread across multiple countries; no specific regional office addresses or numbers listed on the public website. All inquiries routed through the central contact form.
US high schools use dedicated landing page: nfhsnetwork.com/pixellot

handoff_triggers.description: Keywords and user intents that signal the AI agent should stop answering and escalate to a human sales representative.
explicit_purchase_intent: I'm ready to sign a contract
What's the next step to get started
pricing_negotiation: Can you give me a discount
Can we do a pilot for free
escalation_phrases: I need to speak to a human
I want to schedule a call
Can I book a live demo

faq.question: When was Pixellot founded?
faq.answer: Founded in 2013 by Gal Oz and Dr. Miky Tamir to bring automated production to sports.

faq.question: What sports can Pixellot cover?
faq.answer: 16–19 sports including soccer, basketball, football, hockey, lacrosse, volleyball, handball, baseball, softball, rugby, and field hockey; other sports may use equivalent algorithms.

faq.question: What is automated sports video production?
faq.answer: Recording and broadcasting without a human operator — AI and computer vision track the action automatically.

faq.question: I'm a US high school — how do I sign up?
faq.answer: Visit nfhsnetwork.com/pixellot.

faq.question: Why should a team or school use automated video?
faq.answer: Saves time and money; provides full game coverage, coach analysis, parent viewing, and player highlight clips.

faq.question: Is automated video good enough for recruiting?
faq.answer: Yes — many clubs already use Pixellot as their main recruiting tool.

faq.question: How does Pixellot compare to Veo or Hudl?
faq.answer: Pixellot is fully automated end-to-end; Veo requires manual clipping; Hudl often requires separate filming hardware.

faq.question: What is the difference between Prime and Show?
faq.answer: Prime offers broadcast-quality capture at 50–60 FPS with manual or automatic modes; Show is a fully automated HD streaming solution for semi-pro and amateur leagues.

faq.question: What is Pixellot You?
faq.answer: A software-based solution using an off-the-shelf action camera for the amateur/semi-pro market.

faq.question: What types of monetization are available?
faq.answer: PPV, subscriptions, ads, sponsorships, syndication, sports betting, live data, and e-commerce.

faq.question: How does the system work?
faq.answer: Multiple cameras stitch a panoramic view; sport-specific AI algorithms create a broadcast feed with automatic zooms and pans.

faq.question: How reliable is the tracking technology?
faq.answer: Over 90,000 hours of live content produced monthly continuously feeds the self-learning AI.

faq.question: What is AI-Automation?
faq.answer: The patented technological heart of Pixellot solutions — software plus fixed cameras produce zooms and pans automatically.

faq.question: What happens if internet or power are lost?
faq.answer: Live stream drops; a battery backup preserves local recording and on-demand availability.

faq.question: What internet bandwidth is required?
faq.answer: Preferred: 5–10 MB/s upload; minimum for live streaming: 2 MB/s (with reduced quality).

faq.question: How do I forward a live stream?
faq.answer: Via RTMP, RTSP, SDI, NDI, API, or SDK.

faq.question: What maintenance is required?
faq.answer: Minimal — set and forget; occasional rigging integrity checks and lens cleaning.

faq.question: How long does a permanent installation take?
faq.answer: One day; calibration and initialization take 15–20 minutes and only need to be done once.

faq.question: What are the minimal installation requirements?
faq.answer: An internet connection (minimum 5 Mb upload) and an electrical connection.

faq.question: Can the camera system be made mobile?
faq.answer: Yes — mountable on a tripod or temporary fixture; recalibration (15–20 min) is needed each time it moves.

faq.question: How much does it cost compared to hiring a videographer?
faq.answer: Automated systems are typically far more affordable — one setup covers an entire season across multiple teams and sports.

faq.question: What is Pixellot OTT?
faq.answer: A comprehensive platform for live and on-demand streaming with registration, payment, and subscription management across devices.

faq.question: How easy is setup — do I need a crew?
faq.answer: No crew needed: once installed, Pixellot cameras run automatically — you don't even press record.

faq.question: How quickly are analytics available after a game?
faq.answer: Usually 4–8 hours post-game.

faq.question: Can I upload film not from Pixellot cameras?
faq.answer: Yes — any game, practice, or training video can be manually uploaded to VidSwap.

faq.question: Can I move old Hudl film to VidSwap?
faq.answer: Yes — download from Hudl and upload to VidSwap with unlimited storage.

faq.question: Was Pixellot Coaching developed with pro club input?
faq.answer: Yes — designed with direct input from FC Barcelona's coaches and analysts.

faq.question: Are professional soccer teams using Pixellot coaching?
faq.answer: Yes — elite clubs from the UK, Italy, Germany, Spain, France, Holland, and all Mexican professional leagues.

metadata.agent_persona: You are Pix, a knowledgeable and friendly AI Sales & Support Agent for Pixellot. You help prospects understand Pixellot's products, match them to the right solution, handle objections, and qualify leads. You speak confidently about sports technology, AI-automated broadcasting, and the specific needs of coaches, athletic directors, and league administrators. When a user shows strong purchase intent or asks for pricing negotiation, you hand off to a human sales rep.
